key_takeaways: GE Vernova reaffirmed full-year 2025 guidance despite $300M–$400M in estimated tariff and inflation headwinds, citing strong pricing power, contractual protections, and accelerated G&A cost transformation as mitigating actions.

Gas Power demand remains exceptionally strong with 29 GW in backlog and 21 GW in slot reservation agreements (50 GW total under contract/reservation), with 2026–2027 largely sold out and 2028 nearly full; data centers represent ~1/3 of slot reservations.

Q1 2025 free cash flow reached ~$1 billion (up $1.6B year-over-year), driven by higher down payments, improved working capital linearity, and EBITDA growth, while total backlog grew to $123 billion and the company returned $1.3B to shareholders in the quarter.
risk_flags: Tariff exposure estimated at $300M–$400M net of mitigations; ~25% of ~$20B annual direct spend now affected, with China tariffs representing the largest single impact.
Offshore Wind losses persist, including a ~$70M one-time supply agreement termination charge in Q1; tariff costs expected to weigh further on Offshore Wind in Q2.
Onshore Wind orders fell 43% year-over-year due to U.S. policy uncertainty, permitting delays, and higher interest rates, with no clear near-term inflection point identified.
Permitting reform progress likely delayed until 2026 as legislative bandwidth is consumed by tax policy discussions in 2025.
Slot reservation agreements (~21 GW) carry timing risk as customers finalize EPC contracts and site selection, potentially causing slot movement even if cancellation risk is deemed low.
